query_normalized: investigating the impact of code ownership on llm code understanding computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on general software quality metrics (Lines of Code, execution time, object-oriented concepts) and does not measure "code ownership" (a social/organizational construct) nor its specific impact on "LLM code understanding" (a machine learning performance metric). It fails to satisfy any inclusion criteria (a-f) as it lacks the necessary variables, domain (LLMs), or mechanism to be considered relevant prior work for this specific intersection.
